role: Former Minister of Intelligence (1997–1999); former Prosecutor General
bio: Qorbanali Dorri-Najafabadi was [Mohammad Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami)'s first Intelligence Minister, appointed in 1997 as a compromise candidate after hardliners blocked Khatami's preferred choice. His tenure coincided with the 1998 'chain murders' — the serial killing of reformist intellectuals, writers, and political activists by rogue elements of the [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence). When evidence implicated the ministry itself, Dorri-Najafabadi resigned in 1999. He later served as Iran's Prosecutor General (2008–2009) and as a member of the [Assembly of Experts](/orgs/assembly-of-experts). He was a member of the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) before his intelligence appointment.
editorial_notes: Born 1947. Prior to Intelligence Ministry was an MP and member of the Guardian Council. Resigned as Intelligence Minister 1999 following chain murders scandal.
